segments.speaker: Guide
segments.text: This block shows several layers of Boston's urban history.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Can I ask a quick question about the materials?

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Was the brick chosen mainly for preservation reasons, or was it also about matching the surrounding context?

segments.speaker: Guide
segments.text: Both. The review board cared about continuity, but the architects also wanted a quieter street presence.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: That makes sense. It feels new, but it doesn't shout.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: Yes, I'm starting a PhD in architecture, so I'm trying to learn the city by walking it.

segments.speaker: Guide
segments.text: That's the best way to learn Boston.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I'm also curious how the city balances preservation with development pressure.

segments.speaker: You
segments.text: I can already feel that. It's a very useful city to study.
